cards.back: A group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or intervention being tested, used as a baseline to compare the effects of the treatment.
The process of assigning participants or elements to an experiment with randomization to minimize bias and ensure that the groups are comparable.
A measure of the spread or dispersion of data from the mean value, used in analysis of variance (ANOVA) to determine the significance of the treatment effect.
The act of repeating an experiment multiple times to increase the validity and reliability of the results, and to reduce the impact of random chance.

A technique used to prevent bias by keeping certain information (e.g. the treatment being tested) unknown to some or all of the participants or experimenters, such as in medical or psychological studies.
